steps.title: Expand each part
steps.content: First expand the squared binomial and the linear terms: $$(2x+5)^2=4x^2+20x+25,$$ $$-(x-2)=-x+2,$$ and $$2(x+3)=2x+6.$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Add the parts together: $$4x^2+20x+25-x+2+2x+6.$$ The $x$ terms give $20x-x+2x=21x$, and the constants give $25+2+6=33$. The expression is $$4x^2+21x+33,$$ so A is correct.
